Document:
# Skill System
The Skill System allows characters to specialize in different combat and magic styles. Each skill tree provides unique bonuses and abilities.
## Skill Trees
Skill trees define what abilities a character can learn. Your species determines which trees you have access to. For example, Elves start with the Metamagic tree for magic enhancement.
## How to Gain Skill Points
Skill points are the currency for learning new abilities. There are two types of skill points:
[bold]Memory Points[/bold] — used by all standard skill trees (Pyrokinetic, Healing, Metamagic, etc.). Currently these cannot be obtained from items and are only granted through special events or progression.
[bold]Blood Essence[/bold] — used exclusively by the Vampire skill tree. Vampires gain Blood points by: - Being selected as a vampire antagonist (start with 2 Blood points) - Consuming [bold]Blood Essence[/bold] items - Gathering essence from victims
Different skill trees require different point types. Most trees use Memory, while the Vampire tree uses Blood.
## Learning Skills
Open your character menu (press C) and navigate to the Skills tab. Available skills appear in the tree. Click on a skill to learn it if you have enough points.
[bold]Requirements:[/bold] - Available skill points (Memory for standard trees, Blood for Vampire) - Skill must be in an available tree (determined by species) - Some skills require prerequisites (previous skills in the tree)
## Free Skills (Species Bonus)
Some species have innate skills that are learned automatically at character creation. These skills are free and do not require skill points.
[bold]Example:[/bold] - Elf: Metamagic T1 and T2 provide +1 Intelligence bonus each - These apply automatically when the character spawns
## Skill Effects
Skills can provide various bonuses: - [bold]Stat Modifiers:[/bold] +1 to Strength, Vitality, Dexterity, or Intelligence - [bold]Special Abilities:[/bold] Unique combat or magic effects
[bold]Note:[/bold] Stat modifiers from skills contribute to your effective stats and may cause overflow if they push your stat above 10. In such cases, you receive bonus available points instead.
## Related Systems
- [textlink="Character Stats" link="OE14CharacterStats"] — stats affected by skills - [textlink="Combat" link="OE14Combat"] — combat skills for damage bonuses - [textlink="Magic" link="OE14Magic"] — magic skills enhance spellcasting
